title: techmap creates $_MUX_ cell with 1'bx input (correct, but less efficient)
body:
## Steps to reproduce the issue

`./yosys -p "proc; techmap; opt; dump" case1.v`

Minimised from https://github.com/cliffordwolf/picorv32/blob/e0baf2e0bd49fdddef2e3440c1f6364478655154/picorv32.v#L1110-L1124
```
module top(input instr_lui, instr_auipc, instr_jal, output reg decoded_imm);
    always @*
            (* parallel_case *)
            case (1'b1)
                instr_jal:
                        decoded_imm <= 1'b0;
                |{instr_lui, instr_auipc}:
                        decoded_imm <= 1'b1;
                default:
                        decoded_imm <= 1'bx;
            endcase
endmodule
```

## Expected behavior

My understanding is that `opt_expr` is expensive to run, particularly on a bit blasted netlist. We should avoid emitting `1'bx` if possible?

## Actual behavior

![case1](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/6740044/59864647-29326480-933c-11e9-8e30-dd5d2c1289d3.png)
